name: CFSC Checks Cashed 45th St
description_short: CFSC on 45th St offers check cashing, money transfers, bill payments, and prepaid cards without requiring a bank account. Located in Midtown Manhattan with extended weekday hours.
description_long:
CFSC (Community Financial Services) operates a check-cashing location at 158 E 45th St in Midtown Manhattan, positioned as a convenient alternative to traditional banks for customers who lack bank accounts or prefer faster cash access. The company is part of a larger CFSC network with over 20 locations across New York, New Jersey, and Queens, indicating established infrastructure and multiple service points.

The 45th St location offers a comprehensive suite of financial services beyond basic check cashing, including money transfers to over 80 countries, electronic bill pay, prepaid debit cards, money orders, ATM access, foreign currency exchange, prepaid cellular services, Transit/MetroCard purchases, and tax refund check cashing. The business model targets unbanked and underbanked consumers who need fast, accessible financial services without account requirements or waiting periods for checks to clear.

This location distinguishes itself through extended weekday hours (8:00 am–6:30 pm Mon-Fri, 8:00 am–5:00 pm Sat), multilingual staff (Se Habla Español), and centralized Midtown location convenient to office workers and commuters. The company accepts multiple forms of government-issued photo ID and requires only personal contact information and SSN verification, streamlining the check-cashing process compared to traditional banks.

However, the website provides no fee schedules, APRs for prepaid cards, foreign exchange rates, or pricing comparisons—critical information for cost-conscious consumers. The company's fee structure for check cashing (typically 1-5% depending on check type) is not disclosed, and there are no customer reviews, ratings, or transparency about regulatory compliance visible on the location page. This is a transactional service provider rather than a credit-building or wealth-management solution.
pros: No bank account required to cash checks—only valid photo ID and personal information needed
Same-day access to check funds without clearing period delays typical at banks
Extended weekday hours (8:00 am–6:30 pm) accommodate working professionals and commuters
Multilingual staff (Se Habla Español) serves diverse Midtown Manhattan population
Multi-service convenience: 10+ services available in single location including bill pay, money transfers, and prepaid cards
Foreign currency exchange available for 80+ countries, useful for international transfers
Part of large CFSC network (20+ locations) provides alternative access points across NY/NJ region
cons: No fee schedule or pricing information disclosed on website—check cashing costs opaque to consumers
No customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality visible
Prepaid card offerings lack APR, feature details, and cost comparisons to traditional banking alternatives
Limited regulatory transparency—no mention of licensing, compliance certifications, or guarantees
Saturday hours curtailed (8:00 am–5:00 pm) compared to weekday availability; closed Sundays
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked individuals without access to traditional bank accounts
International workers and immigrants needing fast money transfers to 80+ countries
Gig economy workers and contractors requiring same-day check cashing without bank delays
Midtown Manhattan office workers and commuters seeking convenient, after-hours financial services
diagnosis: Best for unbanked or underbanked consumers in Midtown Manhattan who need fast check cashing and multi-service financial access without a bank account. Primary caveat: no fee transparency, no customer reviews, and opaque pricing structure make cost comparison difficult—consumers should ask about check cashing percentages and prepaid card fees before using services.
services: Phone resale (EcoATM kiosk at select locations)
